enonce.texte: Match the words in the left column with their correct definitions or examples in the right column. Draw a line to connect them.

sousQuestions.texte: Breakfast
sousQuestions.reponse: d. The first meal of the day, eaten in the morning.

sousQuestions.texte: Protein
sousQuestions.reponse: c. A nutrient found in meat, fish, and beans that helps build muscles.

sousQuestions.texte: Carbohydrates
sousQuestions.reponse: a. Nutrients found in bread, rice, and pasta that give us energy.

sousQuestions.texte: Vitamins
sousQuestions.reponse: e. Nutrients found in fruits and vegetables that help us stay healthy.

sousQuestions.texte: Lunch
sousQuestions.reponse: b. The meal eaten in the middle of the day.

enonce.texte:
Look at the list of food items below. Classify them into the correct food groups: Fruits, Vegetables, Grains, Dairy, Meat.

Food items: apple, chicken, bread, milk, carrot, rice, cheese, fish, banana, eggs, spinach, pasta, yoghurt

questions.texte: Classify the food items into the correct food groups.
questions.reponse: Fruits: apple, banana; Vegetables: carrot, spinach; Grains: bread, rice, pasta; Dairy: milk, cheese, yoghurt; Meat: chicken, fish, eggs.

sousQuestions.texte: List all the fruits from the list.
List all the grains from the list.

enonce.texte:
Read the short text below about a balanced diet and answer the questions.

A balanced diet is important for good health. It includes different types of food. You should eat carbohydrates for energy, proteins for growth, and vitamins to protect your body. You also need dairy products for strong bones and teeth. Drink plenty of water every day. Try to eat fruit and vegetables in every meal.

questions.reponse: A diet that includes different types of food for good health.

questions.texte: What should you drink every day?

enonce.texte: Look at the pictures and write the name of the meal (breakfast, lunch, dinner, or snack) under each picture.
images: A picture of a bowl of cereal with milk and a glass of orange juice.
A picture of a sandwich with a salad and a bottle of water.
A picture of a roasted chicken with vegetables and mashed potatoes.
A picture of a banana and a handful of almonds.

questions.texte: Identify the meal in each picture.

sousQuestions.texte: Picture 1
Picture 2
Picture 3
Picture 4

enonce.texte: Answer the following questions about healthy eating in one or two words.

questions.texte: Name one food that gives you energy.
Name one food that helps you grow.
What should you drink to stay healthy?

intitule: Vocabulary in Context: Meals and Nutrients

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct words from the box.

Word box: breakfast, lunch, dinner, protein, carbohydrates, vitamins

questions.reponse: 1. breakfast, 2. dinner, 3. vitamins, 4. carbohydrates, 5. lunch, 6. protein

sousQuestions.texte: I eat my ________ in the morning before school.
The biggest meal of the day is usually ________.
Fruits are full of ________, which protect our bodies from sickness.
Bread and rice are good sources of ________.
At noon, I eat my ________ at the canteen.
Fish, eggs, and beans contain ________, which helps build muscles.

enonce.texte:
Read the following simple recipe for fruit salad and answer the questions.

Simple Fruit Salad Recipe: You need two apples, a banana, a handful of grapes, some orange juice, and a spoon of honey. First, wash the fruit. Then, cut the apples and the banana into small pieces. Put them in a bowl. Next, add the grapes. Add the orange juice and honey to the bowl. Finally, mix everything gently. Enjoy!

questions.texte: What is the first step in the recipe?
What do you add in the final step?

enonce.texte: You are planning a healthy menu for your family's dinner. Choose one food item from each food group (Fruits, Vegetables, Grains, Dairy, Meat) and write a complete dinner menu of three items.

questions.texte: Write a balanced dinner menu by choosing one item from each food group.
questions.reponse: Example: Grilled chicken (Meat) with rice (Grains) and a side of steamed broccoli (Vegetables).

sousQuestions.texte: Choose a meat item for your dinner.
Choose a grain item to go with your meat.
Choose a vegetable item to complete the meal.

enonce.texte: Design a healthy menu for one day. Your menu must include breakfast, lunch, and dinner. For each meal, write what you will eat and drink.

questions.texte: Write the menu for one day, including all three meals.
questions.reponse: Example: Breakfast: Oatmeal with milk and a banana, and a glass of water. Lunch: A chicken sandwich with cheese and lettuce, an apple, and water. Dinner: Grilled fish with rice and a side of cooked carrots.

sousQuestions.texte: What will you eat for breakfast? Include a grain and a fruit.
What will you eat for lunch? Include a protein and a vegetable.
What will you eat for dinner? Include a protein and a grain.

intitule: Analyzing a Meal: Is it Healthy?

enonce.texte:
Read the description of the meal below and answer the questions.

Kemi's meal: A large portion of fried plantains with a side of fried eggs. She drinks a bottle of soda.

questions.reponse: No, because the food is fried and the soda has a lot of sugar.

sousQuestions.texte: Are there fruits or vegetables?
What could Kemi change to make the meal healthier?
sousQuestions.reponse: She could bake or grill the plantains instead of frying them, and add a salad.

intitule: Role Play: Planning a Healthy Breakfast

enonce.texte: Work with a classmate. You will create a short dialogue where one person asks for ideas for a healthy breakfast, and the other gives suggestions. Use vocabulary from the lesson.

questions.texte: Create and present a dialogue (at least 4 exchanges) about planning a healthy breakfast.
questions.reponse: Example: A: I need to plan a healthy breakfast. What do you recommend? B: You should eat carbohydrates for energy. A: OK, like what? B: You can eat a bowl of whole-grain cereal with milk. A: That sounds good. I will also add some banana for vitamins.

sousQuestions.texte: What is one piece of advice given in your dialogue?

intitule: Project: Creating a Balanced Diet Poster

enonce.texte: Create an educational poster for your school's Health Club. The poster should explain what a balanced diet is and show examples of healthy foods from each food group. Use at least 5 new vocabulary words from the lesson.

questions.texte: Create and describe your poster for the Health Club. Write 5-6 sentences about what it will show.
questions.reponse: My poster explains a balanced diet. It shows the five food groups: fruits, vegetables, grains, dairy, and meat. It has examples like apples, carrots, bread, milk, and chicken. It explains that carbohydrates give energy. It also says proteins help us grow. Finally, it tells students to drink water.

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph of 5-6 sentences describing what you eat in a typical day. Include what you eat for breakfast, lunch, and dinner, and explain why your meals are healthy or not.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph describing a typical day's meals.
questions.reponse: For breakfast, I eat cereal with milk, which gives me carbohydrates for energy. At lunch, I have a meat sandwich with cheese and lettuce. I drink a glass of water. For dinner, I eat rice with chicken and cooked peas. I think my meals are healthy because they include different food groups. I should eat more fruits, though.
